concepts.description: Prajāpati/Indra/Soma myth as operational script replayed in ritual and somatic practice; same pattern runs at cosmic, ritual, and bodily scales

concepts.title: Skambha and the Cosmo-Body
concepts.description: body as literal cosmos (spine=Skambha axis, cranial vault=sky, cakras=planetary stations); structural homology, not metaphor; AtharvaVeda as primary text

concepts.title: Ātmayajña — The Self-Sacrifice Framework
concepts.description: Vedic sacrifice fully internalized; sacrificer/victim/fire collapsed into one entity; warrior reclaims the rite from priestly intermediary

concepts.title: Prāṇāgnihotra — Breath as Continuous Sacrifice
concepts.description: every breath is already the Vedic fire sacrifice; five vāyus as operational vocabulary; mastering udāna controls the death-exit

concepts.title: Rasa Management — The Fluid Alchemy
concepts.description: body's essential fluid (sweat→ojas→semen→amṛta); default direction is outward/downward; alchemical work reverses this upward through sushumna

concepts.title: Paśu-Virā-Siddha Spectrum
concepts.description: three developmental positions (bound/fighting/liberated); ratio of consciousness to automaticity as the single variable; maps to guṇa triad

concepts.title: Siddha Alchemy — The Nāth Alchemical System
concepts.description: Nāth synthesis of metallurgical alchemy and haṭha yoga; mercury=semen homology; body as philosopher's stone; vajradeha (diamond body) as completion

concepts.title: Vrātya Vocation — The Consecrated Warrior on the Margin
concepts.description: warrior-initiates structurally outside the social order; Rudra-Śiva as patron; vrata (oath) as binding structure; liminal zone as the transformation site; PIE Werh etymology (Vrata = Word/oath), VrataPati as Author of All, Wild Hunt cognate, mortal Ghost Division instantiation added 2026-04-21

concepts.title: Trika Philosophy
concepts.description: non-dual Shaiva; Shiva/Shakti/Nara; 36-tattva partial map; three nāḍīs; five vāyus; Kuṇḍalinī; Zoroastrian comparative; daēnā/Perfect Nature/Sākṣī/Śakti convergence; Āgamic 10+18+64 structure; Paramādva; Lakṣmīdhara/Basaraya split

concepts.title: Yantra as Technology
concepts.description: yantra as voltage transformer; yantra/mantra/tantra trinity; Vāc origin myth; geometry/material/sound/consecration all required; parallel battlespace extension added (mantra-yantra transmission interface as Bagalamukhi's operative target) 2026-04-22

concepts.title: Naga Beings and Culture
concepts.description: non-human Naga beings; human Naga tribes; pre-Vedic Tantric transmission; geographically traceable lineage

concepts.title: Ashta Siddhis
concepts.description: eight classical powers; psychological and social manifestations in Bhairava Sadhana; two-error framework; Gupta Sadhak principle; Kali cosmological hierarchy

concepts.title: Śaiva Theodicy and Leelā
concepts.description: four-level theodicy (skeptical theism → purposive → aesthetic leelā → non-dual dissolution); chamatkāra as Śaiva orientation to divinity; Sankhya-Yoga as bedrock; svātantrya

concepts.title: Chamatkāra — Aesthetic Rapture
concepts.description: aesthetic rapture as the distinctively Śaiva mode of encountering the divine; Natarāja/Ādiyogi/gana iconography; Abhinavagupta's double project (Trika + rasa theory); chamatkāra and recognition

concepts.title: Sankhya-Yoga as Shaiva Foundation
concepts.description: Sankhya (puruṣa/prakṛti/kaivalya) and Yoga (Īśvara as first guru; samādhi method) as Śaivism's philosophical bedrock; svātantrya as the key Śaiva addition transforming Sankhya's divorce into recognition

concepts.title: Paramādva — Maximum Inclusion
concepts.description: the stance that all revelation is Śiva's svātantrya; hierarchy provisional and contextual; Abhinavagupta (historical) and Ramakrishna (modern) as exemplars; not pluralism but maximum freedom

concepts.title: Shiva Siddhānta
concepts.description: South Indian dualist Śaivism; three pāśas (āṇava/māyā/karma); liberation as eternal proximity not dissolution; 28 Āgamas as textual basis; Tamil temple ritual infrastructure; Nāyaṉmār poets

concepts.title: Pratya / Abhijñā — Recognition Not Attainment
concepts.description: liberation as recovery not acquisition; "anything that started must end"; Shakuntala ring as model; mala/kañcuka as obstruction; guru as instrument of recognition

concepts.title: Bhāva vs. Tattva — Experience vs. Reality of God
concepts.description: different liberation models are genuinely different experiences (not different roads to the same place); ghana vs. vijñāna; the Bakta's eternal relationship; Ramakrishna illustration

concepts.title: Guru Tattva and Dīkṣā
concepts.description: guru as principle vs. person; dīkṣā as state-transmission (spiritual contagion); "you become what you contemplate"; āchārya vs. guru distinction; Abhinavagupta pen portrait; Āgamic structure

concepts.title: Karma and Samskaras
concepts.description: karma as physics; samskaras; sacrifice-as-debt model (ātmayajña); Činvat Bridge (fifth tradition on moral accounting); prarabdha; ruṇānubandana; pitru dosha; Charaka navigation principle

concepts.title: Zoroastrian Manifold Soul
concepts.description: seven-component soul; daēnā as karmic mirror; Fravaṣ̌i as Hegemonikon equivalent; Body of Light goal; cross-tradition six-body map

concepts.title: Iranian Illuminationism
concepts.description: Sohravardī's light ontology; Perfect Nature = daēnā = Sākṣī = Śakti (four-tradition convergence); Black Light; three-stage ascent; Kobrā's three nafs; Semnānī's seven latīfa; Shaykhī Body of Diamond

concepts.title: Sufi Fana and Suffering
concepts.description: fana → baqāʾ; Sharīʿa/Ṭarīqa/Ḥaqīqa; three nafs = guṇa-Bhava (fifth tradition); baqāʾ = mokṣa (Burckhardt); Safavid suppression; Zoroastrian-Sufi lineage

concepts.title: Ancestor Veneration — Vedic Framework
concepts.description: Pitru Paksha; mutual evolution model; prarabdha karma; bhūta vs. pitru; pitru dosha; Shraddha food caution (Vimalananda); **CRITICAL LIMIT: transcript cut off before ritual mechanics**

title: ShivaJi, Jai Singh, and Combat Theology (8 pages, NEW 2026-04-25)

concepts.title: Bhavani: The Confirming Goddess
concepts.description: regional Marathi goddess who confirms readiness and promise; darshan as operational shift from faith to certainty; 27-generation promise confirmation

concepts.title: Darshan as Operational Confirmation
concepts.description: direct goddess encounter shifting certainty; somatic reorganization enabling impossible operations; distinction from enlightenment and conversion

concepts.title: 27-Generation Covenant Theology
concepts.description: specific time-bound promise (not infinite); theological sophistication of numbered duration; impacts strategy and risk tolerance

concepts.title: Dynasty as Covenant Entity
concepts.description: lineage as theological structure backed by goddess; transcends individual rulers; 27-generation promise as binding framework

concepts.title: Restraint as Divine Principle
concepts.description: Bagalamukhi as goddess of binding, restraining, holding in place; distinct from protection (Durga) and destruction (Kali)

concepts.title: Invocation as Consciousness Anchor
concepts.description: goddess invocation stabilizes nervous system and intention; enables operations that doubt would prevent

concepts.title: Coalition Strategy vs. Direct Invocation Strategy
concepts.description: Kali theology (bold, concentrated, rapid) vs. Bagalamukhi theology (patient, distributed, steady); two fundamentally different strategic doctrines

concepts.title: Night Raid as Sacred Template
concepts.description: Vedic pattern (Ashvatthama/Kalaratri) as transferable operational doctrine; goddess-authorized infiltration; ritual space reversal
